title: vitis adnata
intro: Vitis adnata is a species of grapevine native to the Himalayan region. It is a climbing plant that belongs to the family Vitaceae and is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Vitis adnata include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating various skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. The plant is typically prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves and roots are used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various ailments.
